features.summary: Lilly reported strong Q3 2021 results with 18% revenue growth driven entirely by volume, reaching $6.8 billion. Key growth products now represent nearly 60% of core business, with standout performance from Trulicity, Verzenio, and Jardiance. The company achieved significant pipeline milestones including FDA approvals for Verzenio in early breast cancer and Jardiance in heart failure, plus regulatory submissions for tirzepatide in diabetes. Operating margin expanded to 30.5%, up over 400 basis points year-over-year, while the company returned nearly $800 million to shareholders through dividends.
features.management_outlook: Management raised 2021 revenue guidance to $27.2-27.6 billion, primarily due to additional COVID-19 antibody revenue of roughly $1.3 billion. The company expects continued margin expansion in Q4 and remains confident in long-term growth prospects despite upcoming headwinds from Alimta patent expiry. For 2022, management anticipates minimal COVID-19 therapy revenue, full-year impact of Alimta generics, and continued investments in promising pipeline opportunities including potential launches of tirzepatide and donanemab. The company maintains its goal of reaching mid-to-high 30s operating margins, though not in linear fashion.
features.risks: Key risks include the full impact of Alimta's US patent expiry in 2022 with generic competition starting in Q2, creating difficult year-over-year comparisons. The Alzheimer's market faces significant infrastructure challenges around diagnostics, infusion capacity, and reimbursement, with CMS's national coverage determination creating uncertainty. COVID-19 therapy revenue will largely disappear in 2022, and continued investments in pipeline development may pressure near-term operating margins. The company also faces ongoing pricing pressures with expected mid-single-digit net price erosion globally.
features.key_drivers: Volume growth remains the primary driver, with key growth products collectively up over 35% and representing 58% of core business revenue. Tirzepatide shows exceptional promise with regulatory submission using priority review voucher, targeting mid-2022 approval for diabetes and 2024 for obesity. Donanemab's rolling BLA submission for Alzheimer's disease represents a major opportunity, with accelerated approval expected in second half 2022 followed by Phase 3 data in mid-2023. Verzenio's new adjuvant breast cancer indication and lebrikizumab's positive Phase 3 atopic dermatitis data provide additional growth catalysts for 2022-2023.
